title: Platform limits shape the design
description: Timeout, memory, payload size, /tmp size, and account concurrency are hard ceilings that force architectural choices, not tunables to ignore.
body: Use when a function fails under scale or large input. FaaS platforms impose hard limits: max execution time, max memory, max request/response payload, ephemeral storage size, and account/function concurrency. Exceeding them causes throttling, truncation, or termination, not graceful degradation. Do not treat limits as advisory. Actions: offload large payloads to object storage and pass references, split long work into steps/state machines, and reserve concurrency to protect downstreams. Validation signals include load tests staying within limits and no throttle/timeout errors. Risk boundary: hitting a concurrency ceiling can throttle an entire account's functions — model burst behavior before deploying high-fan-out workloads.
